example: The delay will affect morale, and its effect is already visible.
Everyone accepted the terms except the youngest officer.

difference: 'Adapt' = adjust to; 'Adopt' = take on as your own
example: Troops must adapt quickly and adopt the new tactics.
The principal reason he resigned was a matter of principle.
The convoy stayed stationary while a clerk fetched fresh stationery.
The new radios complement our kit, and the general paid us a compliment.
The war council met to seek the general's counsel.
He was eligible for promotion, but his handwriting was illegible.
The eminent strategist warned that an attack was imminent.
Her ingenious plan fooled even the ingenuous new recruits.
Tighten that loose strap or you will lose your kit.
They crossed the desert dreaming of a proper dessert.
He cited the manual and marked the ambush site on the map.
The board voted to censure the officer and censor the leaked file.
The confident captain shared the plan only with his confidant.
It was a moral duty to keep the troops' morale high.
Personal disputes among the personnel delayed the mission.
A short briefing will precede the drill; then we proceed.
Engineers had to devise a new device overnight.
I advise caution, so please take my advice.
She stood beside the tank; besides, no one else volunteered.
The base is farther than we thought and needs further supplies.
The order seemed to imply retreat, so we inferred we should fall back.
Lay your rifle down and lie flat on the ground.

difference: 'Allusion' = an indirect reference; 'Illusion' = a false impression
example: His allusion to a quick victory proved to be an illusion.
The coarse terrain made the obstacle course exhausting.
Interrogators tried to elicit details of the illicit deal.
Keep it discreet, and file the reports as discrete documents.
Continual interruptions broke the otherwise continuous watch.
